The **SAFF Women's Championship**, also called the *South Asian Football Federation Women's Cup*, is a competition for women's national [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") teams governed by the [South Asian Football Federation](/wiki/South_Asian_Football_Federation "South Asian Football Federation") (SAFF). All seven members are eligible to participate in the tournament.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SAFF_Women%27s_Championship&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The current SAFF members are [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh_women%27s_national_football_team "Bangladesh women's national football team"), [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan_women%27s_national_football_team "Bhutan women's national football team"), [India](/wiki/India_women%27s_national_football_team "India women's national football team"), [Maldives](/wiki/Maldives_women%27s_national_football_team "Maldives women's national football team"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_women%27s_national_football_team "Nepal women's national football team"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_women%27s_national_football_team "Pakistan women's national football team") and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_women%27s_national_football_team "Sri Lanka women's national football team"). Formerly, eight members used to compete against each other, until [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan_women%27s_national_football_team "Afghanistan women's national football team") departed. It is held approximately every two years. India won first 5 editions, beating Nepal four times and Bangladesh once in the final.[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Overall team records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SAFF_Women%27s_Championship&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Overall team records")]

In this ranking [3 points are awarded for a win](/wiki/Three_points_for_a_win "Three points for a win"), 1 for a draw and 0 for a loss. As per statistical convention in football, matches decided in [extra time](/wiki/Extra_time_(association_football) "Extra time (association football)") are counted as wins and losses, while matches decided by [penalty shoot-outs](/wiki/Penalty_shootout_(association_football) "Penalty shootout (association football)") are counted as draws. Teams are ranked by total points, then by goal difference, then by goals scored.
